content.problemStatement:
You are playing a game called Martingale. The rules of the game are as follows:


At the beginning of the game, you are given NNN as input.
Set V=NV = NV=N, and set X=1X = 1X=1.
Flip a fair coin.

If the coin lands on heads: Increase VVV by XXX, and then set X=1X = 1X=1.
If the coin lands on tails: Decrease VVV by XXX, and then set X=2XX = 2XX=2X.


If V≤0V \leq 0V≤0 or V≥2NV \geq 2NV≥2N:

End the game.


Proceed to step 3.
Given the initial value NNN, what is the expected number of turns (coin flips) you will play before ending the game?


Input Format
Input consists of a single integer, NNN (with 1≤N≤1,000,0001 \leq N \leq 1,000,0001≤N≤1,000,000), the initial input to the game.



Output Format
A single decimal number, containing the expected number of turns you will play this game before it ends. Your output will be accepted if it is within 10−610^{-6}10−6 absolute or relative error.
